mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% per annum, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding and forward guidance effects dominate. Under this regime, further rate cuts signal desperation and economic weakness, reversing the typical easing-positive relationship and producing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.16%, well below 0.50% threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is positive (> 0.05) during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the interaction indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports equity multiples. The joint signal is stronger than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive 1-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 351418.0 million dollars, HOUST at 581.0 thousand units
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 95.0 index points signal supply-side contraction and capacity utilization stress. This constraint on output growth produces stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as earnings growth expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 95.9709, marginally above trigger threshold
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 95.0, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO level and 60-day forward returns is positive (> 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 9.5% signal labor market deterioration and recession risk. This threshold crossing triggers a regime shift where equity investors reprrice tail risk and reduce equity allocations, producing negative 5-day returns as the signal propagates through market microstructure.
regime_hint: labor market stress and recession risk regime
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9.5%, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the magnitude of negative returns in triggered periods is not significantly larger than baseline (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact such that when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%, the real rate becomes deeply negative. This signals monetary accommodation that inflates asset prices, producing positive 20-day equity returns through portfolio rebalancing into risk assets.
regime_hint: negative real rates monetary accommodation regime
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient in a logistic model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This deceleration, observable with a 5-month lag, produces negative 60-day equity returns as investors anticipate earnings downgrades and multiple compression in the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12892.5 billion chained 2017 dollars, asof t-5 months
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is positive (> 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly expand, signaling synchronized supply and labor capacity growth. This interaction indicates sustainable productivity gains and output expansion that support equity valuations through higher earnings per share without wage-driven inflation pressure.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_60d.
